blockingIssues.description: `RealtimeSessionModelSettings::merge()` keeps the prior nested `audio` subtree when an update only uses flat aliases, and `normalize_effective()` then prefers the stale nested values over the new flat aliases. Because normalized settings are persisted back into session/model state, later flat-only updates like `voice`, `speed`, or `output_audio_format` can be ignored, so mixed flat/nested updates still do not normalize to the caller's intended effective settings.

`Runner` resolves trace metadata using only `self.config.model` before it falls back to `Agent.model` for the actual request. With `MultiProvider`, routed OpenAI models declared on `Agent.model` therefore skip the harness metadata hook for top-level trace metadata, leaving `agent_harness_id` absent on that public path even though request routing later succeeds.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update `rust-runtime-worker` to explicitly warn that `cargo test <filter> -- --exact` can exit 0 when no tests match, and tell workers to confirm that the filtered command actually ran the intended test.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The rerun review for `realtime-session-and-transport-parity` and earlier worker handoffs call out zero-match `--exact` runs as a recurring procedure pitfall; the library already documents the quirk, but the worker skill still does not.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a verification-only path to `rust-runtime-worker` for assignments where the feature is already landed or the remaining work is exact regression coverage rather than fresh implementation, so workers do not have to report a false test-first deviation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `session-backend-local-parity` review and handoff show a worker correctly validating already-landed behavior while having to mark a procedural deviation because the skill only describes test-first implementation work.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify when `rust-runtime-worker` requires a manual shell verification for facade-visible behavior and require workers to record an explicit rationale whenever automated cargo validation is sufficient instead.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The reviews for `runtime-conversation-tracking-and-session-input`, `runtime-openai-session-and-runstate-parity`, and `runtime-turn-controls-and-input-filtering` all found the manual-verification rule ambiguous or skipped without being reflected accurately in handoff metadata.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Expand routed harness-metadata validation to cover both model sources used by the public runtime: `RunConfig.model` and `Agent.model` when `MultiProvider` routes OpenAI models.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The rerun review for `trace-and-harness-metadata-parity` found that the current regression only exercises the `RunConfig.model` path, which allowed the remaining `Agent.model` trace-metadata gap to survive.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the `VAL-INTEGRATIONS-018` evidence to reference the new exact regression `transcript_only_runs_skip_tts_when_stream_audio_disabled`, which proves transcript-only voice runs do not depend on TTS success.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The rerun review for `voice-workflow-and-audio-parity` found the contract still cites the older buffered-audio suppression test even though the new exact regression is now the real proof of no-TTS behavior.

rejectedObservations.observation: The knowledge observations from `runtime-default-runner-and-sync-semantics` and `agent-tools-and-approval-flow-parity` about `cargo test <filter> -- --exact` matching already exist in `.factory/library/environment.md`.

The manual-verification observation from `runtime-openai-session-and-runstate-parity` duplicates the same `rust-runtime-worker` guidance gap also reported by `runtime-conversation-tracking-and-session-input` and `runtime-turn-controls-and-input-filtering`.

The manual-verification observation from `runtime-turn-controls-and-input-filtering` duplicates the same `rust-runtime-worker` guidance gap also reported by `runtime-conversation-tracking-and-session-input`.
